section.heading: Shore leave for professional mariners

content.p: The Under Secretary may prescribe regulations relating to shore leave for professional mariners without regard to the requirements of section 6305 of title 5.

subsection.heading: Requirements
subsection.chapeau: The regulations prescribed under subsection (a) shall—

paragraph.content: require that a professional mariner serving aboard an ocean-going vessel be granted a leave of absence of 4 days per pay period; and

provide that a professional mariner serving in a temporary promotion position aboard a vessel may be paid the difference between such mariner’s temporary and permanent rates of pay for leave accrued while serving in the temporary promotion position.

content.p: In this section, the term “professional mariner” means an individual employed on a vessel of the Administration who has the necessary expertise to serve in the engineering, deck, steward, electronic technician, or survey department.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 107–372, title II, § 269B, as added Pub. L. 117–263, div. K, title CXVII, § 11706(a), Dec. 23, 2022, 136 Stat. 4157.)
